description: Cross-client v2 manifest-signing signature lock (batch 6f — A3, audit crypto-correctness H-1). The editor signs the canonical manifest_v2 transcript (mosaic-domain: 'Mosaic_Manifest_v2' || version=2 || album_id(16) || epoch_id_LE_u32(4) || manifest_seq_LE_i64(8) || encrypted_meta_len_LE_u32(4) || encrypted_meta || shard_count_LE_u32(4) || (chunk_index_LE_u32(4) || tier(1) || shard_id(16) || sha256(32))*) with the per-epoch Ed25519 ManifestSigningSecretKey. The 8-byte manifest_seq inserted after epoch_id is the monotonic freshness lever the server and sync client enforce per (album_id, epoch_id) to reject replays of older signed manifests. Ed25519 (RFC 8032) is deterministic given a fixed seed + message, so all three client implementations (Rust, WASM, UniFFI) MUST produce the exact signature bytes locked here. v1 readers will NEVER accept this v2 signature because the context prefix is byte-distinct ('Mosaic_Manifest_v1' vs 'Mosaic_Manifest_v2') — guaranteeing no-silent-downgrade.

negativeCases.name: tampered-manifest-seq
negativeCases.rationale: manifest_seq is bound into the signed bytes; a server replaying an older signed manifest under a newer seq value fails verification (closes audit crypto-correctness H-1).

negativeCases.name: tampered-context-prefix-v1
negativeCases.rationale: The v2 context prefix is byte-distinct from v1; a downgrade attempt to a v1 transcript MUST fail verification (no silent downgrade).

negativeCases.name: tampered-epoch-id
negativeCases.rationale: epoch_id is little-endian-encoded into the transcript; cross-epoch replay fails verification.

negativeCases.name: tampered-signature
negativeCases.rationale: Ed25519 strict verify rejects any modification of the signature bytes.

negativeCases.name: wrong-pubkey
mutations: verify with a different 32-byte pubkey (e.g. all-0x55)
negativeCases.rationale: Cross-key substitution must fail — the sync visitor only trusts the album's published epoch signing pubkey.

serverBoundOutputs.name: transcriptBytes
serverBoundOutputs.reason: Manifest transcript bytes are the message signed by the album epoch.

serverBoundOutputs.name: signature
serverBoundOutputs.reason: Ed25519 signature stored in the manifests row and re-emitted in sync responses.

serverBoundOutputs.name: manifestSeq
serverBoundOutputs.reason: Monotonic freshness counter; the backend enforces strict monotonicity per (album_id, signer_pubkey).

forbiddenServerOutputs.name: signingSeed
forbiddenServerOutputs.reason: The per-epoch signing seed never leaves the client — only its derived pubkey is published.
